# [CHECKLIST TYPE] Checklist: [FEATURE NAME]

**Created**: [DATE]
**Feature**: [Link to spec.md or relevant documentation]

## Code Quality & Maintainability (Principle I)

- [ ] CHK001 All Java code follows Oracle conventions and Checkstyle rules
- [ ] CHK002 HTL templates are semantic with no inline JavaScript
- [ ] CHK003 JavaScript/TypeScript follows ESLint config with strict mode
- [ ] CHK004 CSS/SCSS uses BEM methodology or component-scoped styling
- [ ] CHK005 JavaDoc present for all public APIs (@param, @return, @throws)
- [ ] CHK006 SonarQube quality gates pass (0 blocker/critical, <3% duplication)
- [ ] CHK007 Cyclomatic complexity ≤10 (or justified if higher)
- [ ] CHK008 No deprecated AEM APIs used (or migration path documented)

## Coding Standards & Rule Compliance (Principle II)

- [ ] CHK009 All applicable rule files consulted from .cursor/rules/ before code changes
- [ ] CHK010 Code follows patterns documented in .cursor/rules/
- [ ] CHK011 Backend code adheres to applicable backend rule files (Java, HTL, XML, etc.)
- [ ] CHK012 Frontend code adheres to applicable frontend rule files (JavaScript, CSS, clientlibs)
- [ ] CHK013 Shared rule files applied to both frontend and backend (e.g., frontend/aem_wcag_rules.mdc)
- [ ] CHK014 Component structure follows backend/aem-component-structure.mdc
- [ ] CHK015 Sling Models follow backend/aem-sling-models.mdc patterns
- [ ] CHK016 HTL templates follow frontend/aem-htl-rules.mdc guidelines
- [ ] CHK017 Accessibility patterns from frontend/aem_wcag_rules.mdc applied to HTL and JS
- [ ] CHK018 Dialog configurations follow frontend/aem-dialog-rules.mdc
- [ ] CHK019 Code review includes rule file compliance verification
- [ ] CHK020 No deviations from rule files without documented justification

## Testing Standards (Principle III)

- [ ] CHK021 Tests written BEFORE implementation (test-first workflow)
- [ ] CHK022 Test scenarios approved by stakeholder before implementation
- [ ] CHK023 Unit tests achieve ≥80% line coverage (JUnit 5, Mockito)
- [ ] CHK024 Integration tests cover all OSGi service interactions (AEM Mocks, Sling Mocks)
- [ ] CHK025 Tests organized: unit (src/test/java), integration (digitalxn-aem-ui-tests)

## Security (Principle IV)

- [ ] CHK026 All user input validated against whitelist patterns (regex, allowed values)
- [ ] CHK027 Output encoding applied (HTL context-aware or OWASP Java Encoder)
- [ ] CHK028 AEM built-in authentication leveraged (no custom auth implementations)
- [ ] CHK029 Sling Resource Access Control (ACLs) used for authorization
- [ ] CHK030 CSRF token validation on POST/PUT/DELETE endpoints (Granite CSRF)
- [ ] CHK031 XSS prevention: no innerHTML/dangerouslySetInnerHTML; sanitize with OWASP policies
- [ ] CHK032 No credentials in code (OSGi config with AEM Crypto)
- [ ] CHK033 Dependencies scanned with OWASP Dependency Check
- [ ] CHK034 Dispatcher filters block /apps, /libs, .json selectors
- [ ] CHK035 Content-Type headers explicit (no sniffing)
- [ ] CHK036 HTTPS-only cookies with SameSite=Strict

## Accessibility (Principle V) - WCAG 2.2 AA

- [ ] CHK037 All interactive elements keyboard-accessible with visible focus indicators
- [ ] CHK038 All images have descriptive alt text (or alt="" for decorative)
- [ ] CHK039 All forms have properly associated labels and error messages
- [ ] CHK040 Semantic HTML5 elements and ARIA landmarks used appropriately
- [ ] CHK041 Dynamic content changes announced via ARIA live regions
- [ ] CHK042 Components tested with assistive technologies (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver)
- [ ] CHK043 Axe DevTools scan shows 0 critical or serious issues
- [ ] CHK044 Keyboard-only navigation tested for all user journeys
- [ ] CHK045 New components include accessibility acceptance criteria in specs

## Performance (Principle VI)

- [ ] CHK046 Page load <3s on 3G connection (Lighthouse score ≥85)
- [ ] CHK047 AEM component rendering <200ms (95th percentile)
- [ ] CHK048 JavaScript bundle <200KB gzipped (code-split, lazy-loaded)
- [ ] CHK049 Images optimized and served in WebP with responsive srcset
- [ ] CHK050 Critical CSS inlined, non-critical resources deferred
- [ ] CHK051 Oak queries indexed (execution time <100ms, 95th percentile)
- [ ] CHK052 Client libraries minified with appropriate cache headers
- [ ] CHK053 Performance budgets established and monitored
- [ ] CHK054 Sling Dynamic Include used for personalized fragments
- [ ] CHK055 Component hierarchy ≤5 levels deep
- [ ] CHK056 Static assets served via CDN with long TTLs (1 year for versioned)

## Maintainability & Technical Debt (Principle VII)

- [ ] CHK057 Components follow single responsibility principle; avoid god components
- [ ] CHK058 Dependencies managed explicitly via Maven; no version conflicts
- [ ] CHK059 Configuration externalized via OSGi configs and runmodes
- [ ] CHK060 Deprecated AEM APIs avoided (or migration path documented)
- [ ] CHK061 Logging structured and meaningful (SLF4J); appropriate levels

## Observability (Principle VIII)

- [ ] CHK062 Structured logging with SLF4J and JSON formatter
- [ ] CHK063 Correlation IDs included in logs (request tracking)
- [ ] CHK064 Log levels appropriate (ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG)
- [ ] CHK065 JMX metrics exposed for OSGi services (count, duration, errors)
- [ ] CHK066 Error tracking integrated with monitoring (Splunk, Datadog)
- [ ] CHK067 Audit logging enabled for authoring changes (/var/audit)
- [ ] CHK068 Dispatcher logs monitored (cache hit/miss, response times, 5xx)
- [ ] CHK069 Custom health checks for critical dependencies
- [ ] CHK070 AEM request logs enabled with timing breakdown

## Project Context & Configuration (Principle IX)

- [ ] CHK071 Applicable project(s) identified in spec.md from ./specify/memory/projects/
- [ ] CHK072 Feature designs support multi-project reusability
- [ ] CHK073 Project-specific variations handled via configuration (not hardcoded)
- [ ] CHK074 Implementation plan documents project-specific adaptations
- [ ] CHK075 Task breakdowns include project-specific validation steps
- [ ] CHK076 New projects registered in ./specify/memory/projects/ with documentation
- [ ] CHK077 Cross-project dependencies explicitly declared and validated

## Security & Compliance

### Dependency Management
- [ ] CHK078 Dependencies sourced from approved Azure Artifacts repository
- [ ] CHK079 Dependency versions explicitly declared in parent POM
- [ ] CHK080 Vulnerability scanning runs on every build; critical CVEs fail build
- [ ] CHK081 License compliance verified (GPL prohibited without approval)

### Deployment Security
- [ ] CHK082 Production credentials managed via secure vault; no plaintext passwords
- [ ] CHK083 Deployment artifacts signed and verified
- [ ] CHK084 Environment-specific configs isolated (no prod keys in lower envs)

## Development Workflow

- [ ] CHK085 Feature branch follows naming: feature/###-feature-name
- [ ] CHK086 Review checklist: tests present, security validated, accessibility checked
- [ ] CHK087 Unit tests pass with minimum coverage thresholds
- [ ] CHK088 Integration tests executed successfully
- [ ] CHK089 User-facing documentation included
- [ ] CHK090 Breaking changes include migration guides

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
